posts.name: Richard Flack
posts.cooked:
I’ve always thought that Bret Bros was pretty inauspicious for a Burgundy house, at least for exporting to North America.

Any others of this ilk?

posts.name: Jack Linden
posts.cooked:
Nate Simon:

Domaine de la Pousse d’Or always gives me a chuckle.

“Une pousse d’or” is the French equivalent of “a green thumb” (pousse=thumb).

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Jack Linden:
Nate Simon:

Domaine de la Pousse d’Or always gives me a chuckle.

“Une pousse d’or” is the French equivalent of “a green thumb” (pousse=thumb).

The thing to bear in mind here is that in the USA, since the second language is Spanish, non-Francophone speakers will often pronounce the letter ‘e’ as ‘ay’…
